name: Pico de la Jarra and Hayal de Aloños Loop
name_original: Hayedo de Aloños y Pico de la Jarra – circular por el río Pas
description: This difficult 15.3-mile mountain biking route through Cantabria features the Hayal de Aloños Beech Forest and panoramic views from Pico de
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Santiurde De Toranzo are found within Cantabria's picturesque Valles Pasiegos, characterized by lush green valleys, the Pas River, and varied mountain terrain. The region offers a diverse landscape for mountain biking, including dense beech forests like Hayal de Aloños and rolling hills. This area provides a network of routes that traverse both natural features and historical elements. Best mountain bike trails around Santiurde De Toranzo The most popular mountain bike trail is Pico de la Jarra and Hayal de Aloños Loop, a 15.3 miles (24.7 km) difficult trail that takes 2 hours 54 minutes to complete. This route features the scenic Hayal de Aloños Beech Forest and provides panoramic views from Pico de la Jarra. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Churrón de Borleña y túmulos de Quintana – circular por la Vía Verde del Pas, a moderate 18.6 miles (29.9 km) path. This route incorporates sections of the Pas Greenway, offering a mix of natural and historical scenery. Local mountain bikers also love the La Concha – Cabárceno Obregón Entrance loop from Vargas, a 30.9 miles (49.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain and green valleys, often completed in about 2 hours 58 minutes. Mountain biking around Santiurde De Toranzo is defined by green valleys, the Pas River, and the Montes del Valle de Toranzo, with options for easy rides to challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Santiurde De Toranzo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Santiurde De Toranzo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Santiurde De Toranzo?
A: There are over 70 mountain bike trails around Santiurde De Toranzo, catering to various skill levels. This includes 5 easy routes, 48 moderate routes, and 21 difficult routes, offering a diverse range of experiences in the Valles Pasiegos region.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Santiurde De Toranzo offers 5 easy mountain bike routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40323612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reyerma Steam Locomotive – Puente Viesgo loop from Santiurde de Toranzo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 31.9 km long and suitable for various fitness levels, making it a good option for those looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The trails in Santiurde De Toranzo traverse lush green valleys, follow the beautiful Pas River, and wind through varied mountain terrain. You can also experience dense beech forests like the Hayal de Aloños and enjoy panoramic views from elevated points such as Pico de la Jarra. The region's natural beauty is a significant highlight for mountain bikers.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Santiurde De Toranzo are circular. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000940280/churron-de-borlena-y-tumulos-de-quintana-circular-por-la-via-verde-del-pas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Churrón de Borleña y túmulos de Quintana – circular por la Vía Verde del Pas&lt;/a&gt; is a 29.9 km loop that incorporates sections of the Pas Greenway. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999744021/pico-de-la-jarra-and-hayal-de-alonos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de la Jarra and Hayal de Aloños Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 24.7 km circular route.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can be found along the mountain bike trails?
A: You can encounter several historical features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5156745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pas Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, a former railway line, offers a mix of natural and historical scenery. Some routes also pass by ancient burial mounds like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4847953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintana IV Tumulus&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4847986" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintana II Burial Mound&lt;/a&gt;, providing glimpses into the region's past.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or other unique natural attractions to see?
A: Yes, the area features the notable &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4146541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Churrón de Borleña Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, a beautiful natural attraction that can be accessed via some routes. The Hayal de Aloños Beech Forest also offers a captivating natural environment for riders.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Santiurde De Toranzo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Santiurde De Toranzo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the scenic green valleys, and the challenging options available for more experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by the Pas Greenway?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5156745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pas Greenway&lt;/a&gt;. For instance, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000940280/churron-de-borlena-y-tumulos-de-quintana-circular-por-la-via-verde-del-pas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Churrón de Borleña y túmulos de Quintana – circular por la Vía Verde del Pas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40323829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pas Greenway – Puente Viesgo loop from Santiurde de Toranzo&lt;/a&gt; both offer a relaxed cycling experience along this scenic path.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Santiurde De Toranzo?
A: The Valles Pasiegos region, where Santiurde De Toranzo is located, is known for its lush green landscapes. While specific trail conditions can vary, the area generally offers pleasant riding conditions during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially on shaded forest trails.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 21 difficult routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999744021/pico-de-la-jarra-and-hayal-de-alonos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de la Jarra and Hayal de Aloños Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 24.7 km trail with significant elevation gain, offering panoramic views and technical sections.

Q: Can I find trails that lead to viewpoints or offer scenic vistas?
A: Many trails in the region offer scenic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999744021/pico-de-la-jarra-and-hayal-de-alonos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de la Jarra and Hayal de Aloños Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for example, provides panoramic views from Pico de la Jarra. The diverse terrain of the Montes del Valle de Toranzo also ensures numerous opportunities for breathtaking landscapes throughout your ride.

Q: Are there any nearby attractions suitable for a post-ride visit?
A: Yes, after your ride, you could visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5411623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabárceno Nature Park&lt;/a&gt;, which is a short distance away and offers a unique experience with animals in semi-freedom. Alternatively, explore the historical &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5410560" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reyerma Steam Locomotive&lt;/a&gt; or the natural beauty of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4146541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Churrón de Borleña Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Beautiful beech forest on Mount Hayal (Aloños) that is greatly enjoyed in spring with its beautiful greens, or in autumn when the leaves fall and everything is a festival of ochres, yellows and reddish. Preferably go with suitable footwear because although the road is very wide, it is still a track in the forest that is very wet and there are areas with a lot of mud.

The Pico de la Jarra, also called Tablao (or Tablau), is 820 meters high and the first thing you notice is the fantastic views that this small mountain range has.
It can be easily reached from Rasillo or Aloños, which are from the Carriedo valley or from the Toranzo valley, for example, also passing through the beautiful beech forest of Cotero Lobos... it is an accessible place with spectacular views.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This is a difficult mountain bike route, requiring very good fitness and advanced riding skills. It's rated as STS level S2, with some sections potentially reaching S3-S6, meaning you might need to push your bike. The ascent from Aloños is particularly demanding, with a strong and constant climb.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The route features diverse terrain, including significant portions of unpaved and natural paths, along with about 2.57 km of singletrack. You'll also encounter shorter sections of paved road and access roads. Be prepared for varied surface conditions, which can be wet and muddy, especially after rain.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail's main highlights include the stunning Hayal de Aloños Beech Forest, which is beautiful year-round, especially in autumn. You'll also ascend to Pico de la Jarra (820 meters), offering fantastic panoramic views of the Pasiegos Valleys.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is particularly spectacular in spring when the beech forest is vibrant green, or in autumn for a 'festival of ochres, yellows and reddish' leaves. However, be aware that wet conditions can make the natural paths muddy, so check the weather forecast, especially if riding after rain.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly restricted, given the challenging nature of the mountain bike route, its technical sections, and potential for muddy terrain, it might not be ideal for all dogs. If you do bring a dog, ensure it is well-behaved, accustomed to long, strenuous rides, and kept on a leash, especially in forested areas and near livestock.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Hayedo de Aloños y Pico de la Jarra mountain bike route. It's generally open for public use.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information isn't detailed, but trailheads in this region often have small parking areas in or near the villages closest to the start of the route. It's advisable to look for parking in Aloños or nearby hamlets, respecting local signage and private property.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other official trails. You'll ride along parts of the Camino Natural del Valle de Toranzo (19% of the route), the Vía Verde del Pas (12%), and the Camino Vejaris (2%).

faqs.question: What should I pack for this challenging mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the difficulty and varied terrain, you should pack essential mountain biking gear, including a helmet, appropriate protective clothing, plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit, and a first-aid kit. Due to potential muddy sections, suitable footwear is also important, especially if you anticipate pushing your bike.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or water sources along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a sense of peace and tranquility, immersing you in nature, which means amenities like cafes or designated water sources are scarce directly on the trail. It's best to carry all necessary water and snacks with you. You might find small establishments in nearby villages before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Is this a good trail for beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is not suitable for beginner mountain bikers. It is classified as 'difficult' and requires very good fitness and advanced riding skills due to its significant elevation gain, technical sections (S2-S6), and varied terrain. Beginners should look for easier routes in the region.
